patterns.description: Map skills to development phases via slash commands (/spec, /plan, /build, /test, /review, /ship). Users get instant clarity.
Treat SKILL.md as trainable parameters of a frozen agent. Use rollout→reflect→aggregate→select→update→evaluate loop to improve skills programmatically.
Always provide install instructions for ALL major platforms (Cursor, Claude Code, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI) in collapsible sections. Maximizes reach.
Include quantified scope in repo description: '150+ Skills', '90+ modules', '100+ databases'. Numbers signal depth and value.
Offensive security skills (red team, OSINT, pentesting) consistently get 1K-3K stars. Underserved niche with high demand.
Skills that improve OTHER skills (SkillOpt, agent-skill-creator) are a novel high-value category. The 'skill about skills' niche is growing.
Curated awesome-lists of skills get 10K-146K stars. But require massive ongoing curation. Only pursue if committed to maintenance.
Deep expertise in one domain (science: 27K, security: 1.9K) beats shallow coverage of many domains. Go DEEP in niches.

philosophies.text: Skills encode the workflows that senior engineers use — packaged so AI agents follow them consistently
Treat the skill document as the trainable state of a frozen agent, train it with the discipline of weight-space optimization
The deployed artifact is a compact best_skill.md that runs against the unchanged target model — zero inference-time cost
One SKILL.md, every platform — cross-platform is the standard, not the exception

techniques.name: SkillOpt training loop
techniques.description: rollout → reflect → aggregate → select → update → evaluate. Bounded edits (add/delete/replace). Validation gate. Textual learning rate.

techniques.name: Slash command mapping
techniques.description: Define 5-7 slash commands that map to workflow phases. Skills auto-activate based on current activity.
